steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The word but signals contrast: sculpture is sometimes characterized as idealizing, yet many sculptures depict people differently. Idealizing means presenting subjects as perfect and beautiful.

steps.title: Find what contrasts with idealization
steps.content:
The word needs to mean the opposite of idealizing — depicting subjects as imperfect or unattractive. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — unflattering: presenting someone in an unattractive or unfavorable way — directly contrasts "idealizing," which means making subjects look perfect. ✓
  • Choice B — superficial: lacking depth — contrasts thoroughness, not beauty; the passage is about attractiveness, not depth of treatment. ✗
  • Choice C — imprecise: inaccurate or vague — contrasts accuracy, not idealization; a sculpture can be imprecise yet still flattering. ✗
  • Choice D — implausible: unbelievable or unrealistic — contrasts believability, not idealization; an implausible depiction could still be idealized. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
